content: Just as the educational linguistic definition of reflexion frames it as an intentional metacognitive step to deepen learning by re-examining initial understandings of language and text, the original Reflexion autonomous agent framework can be extended to draw on structured, classroom-style reflexion prompting routines to improve how large language models engage in self-reflection. Specifically, adopting the guided reflective questioning frameworks used in high school writing workshops can help LLM self-reflection mechanisms move beyond inconsistent self-critique to produce more consistent, actionable self-improvement.
